steps.title: Pin down the stated goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize how far the infantrymen traveled. The only note that quantifies distance is the 1,900-mile figure.

steps.title: Look for the choice that foregrounds the distance
steps.content: The correct sentence must include that 1,900-mile detail and put it in a position of emphasis.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — The US infantrymen rode their bicycles from Montana to Missouri—traveling a total of 1,900 miles.: states the distance and highlights it after the em dash. ✓
  • Choice B — The 125th anniversary of the infantrymen's journey was in 2022.: misses the goal — emphasizes timing, not distance. ✗
  • Choice C — The goal of the 1897 journey was to test the idea of forming a military bicycle corps.: misses the goal — emphasizes purpose, not distance. ✗
  • Choice D — Over a century later, Erick Cedeño honored the infantrymen by reenacting their 1897 journey.: misses the goal — emphasizes the reenactment, not how far the infantrymen traveled. ✗
That matches Choice A — The US infantrymen rode their bicycles from Montana to Missouri—traveling a total of 1,900 miles..
